title: Employee Onboarding Program from Scratch
summary: Built the organization's first structured onboarding program — fully automated endpoint provisioning via Autopilot, day-one security awareness training, and identity lifecycle management through Entra ID. New hires are productive and compliant before IT lifts a finger.
problem: Onboarding was ad-hoc. Every new hire kicked off a series of email threads to provision systems, image a laptop, schedule training, and grant access — often days or weeks late. There was no day-one experience, no structured security awareness, and no identity lifecycle to speak of.
approach: Designed a hire-to-active workflow triggered by an HR start-date event in ADP that flows through Entra ID provisioning
Configured Autopilot deployment profiles per role so the laptop ships pre-enrolled, pre-configured, and waiting for the user's first sign-in
Mandated security awareness training as a Conditional Access prerequisite — no training, no sign-in to organization resources
Built a SharePoint intranet onboarding hub with policies, runbooks, and the org chart so day-one questions answer themselves
Tied offboarding to the same lifecycle — termination in HR triggers immediate revocation of identity, device, badge, and licensed application access
lessons: Onboarding programs are easier to design than to maintain — the workflow degrades the moment HR adds a new role or IT changes a deployment profile and nobody updates the linkage. Build a quarterly review into the cadence so the workflow stays in sync with how the organization actually staffs and provisions. Also: making security awareness a Conditional Access gate sounds aggressive but it works — users complete training the first day instead of dragging it out for weeks.
